name: Field Operations Assistant
description: Job Description: In the Philippines, BASF has been supporting manufacturers from various industries since 1963. Today, BASF is a leading supplier of chemicals for industries, including Construction and Coatings, Automotive, Nutrition & Health, Home & Personal Care, and Agriculture. BASF also operates an Agricultural Research Station in the country which conducts research, laboratory studies and field testing of novel technologies. In 2024, BASF posted sales to customers in the Philippines of approximately €100 million and employed 92 people as of the end of the year. Further information is available at www.basf.com/ph . Field Operations Assistant O bjectives of the Position: Assist and help the Field Operations Manager in the smooth implementation of in-house and off-site “Farm Operation Activities” of the Agricultural Research Station (ARS) – Bay Laguna. Main Areas of Responsibilities and Key Activities Assist the Field Operations Manager in the planning, organizing and allocating of resources for the efficient execution of trials/ experiment of the customers: Field Biology Indications (Fungicide, Herbicide and Insecticide) and Insecticide Research (Insectary Team, CTI Team and advance Testing/Microplot Team). Help the Field Operations Manager in creating good communication and interaction with other agricultural research stations, research institutes, universities, and suppliers to gain and share best practice(s). Help in the monitoring and implementation of health and safety policies/procedures, EHS/RCMS/EMS, and DOLE regulations. Job Requirements College Graduate (preferably in Agriculture/Agricultural Engineering) or related Sciences At least 3-5 years of experience handling farm operations Research process, farm operations (i.e., handling farm implements/machineries etc.,) and agronomy (standard procedure in growing plants) Technical knowhow in analyzing the problems and relate solutions to the occurring problems Creative and Innovative – Presence of passion in helping the team to optimize further existing methodologies and procedures Computer applications such as MS Office (Word, Power point, Excel, etc.). Demonstrate professionalism, and integrity at work. Demonstrate high level of energy and maintain stable performance under pressure. Proactive, can work independently and in teams All applications will be treated confidentially. Only short-listed candidates will be interviewed. Job Description Footer:

name: Country Controller
description: In the Philippines, BASF has been supporting manufacturers from various industries since 1963. Today, BASF is a leading supplier of chemicals for industries, including Construction and Coatings, Automotive, Nutrition & Health, Home & Personal Care, and Agriculture. BASF also operates an Agricultural Research Station in the country which conducts research, laboratory studies and field testing of novel technologies. In 2024, BASF posted sales to customers in the Philippines of approximately €100 million and employed 92 people as of the end of the year. Further information is available at www.basf.com/ph . Job Description: Objectives of the Position In charge of financial planning, forecasting, reporting and business analysis and commercial controlling support for APA business in country. Act as financial advisor, challenger and business partner to the APA Local Business Manager (LBM) in all matters of financial relevance including business steering and projects/initiatives. Be the go-to person for Compliance questions. Be member of the country and business area APA management team Main Tasks Financial Reporting and Forecasting & Operational Planning: Responsible for monthly actual reporting and full year forecasting of P&L, specific balance sheet items and sales. Conduct forecasting processes as part of the integrated planning process (supply FC, demand FC, financial FC). Accountable for developing, tracking and reporting financial scenarios (risks & opportunities). Develop business commentary for corporate management. Identify risks/downsides and opportunities/upsides to sales plan. Document and track risks and opportunities including likelihood and decision time and review on a monthly basis. Commercial and Operational Controlling: Collect and review calendarized articles including volumes and prices. Support Country Management in steering pricing and rebate schemes. Develop price/volume scenarios. Check rebate settlement calculation, monitor rebate accruals. Track profitability on product level and initiate countermeasures (e.g. price increases) Track and support steering of expenses, i.e. selling cost, R&D cost, and manufacturing/tolling cost (if applicable) Request for ICTPs of new products, providing relevant information relating to market price and local COGS Monitor development of local COGS development (PUP review, MARS Q&A, material master status, tolling fees). Support operational business steering with adequate reports, ad-hoc analyses and recommendations Drive operational excellence projects Be the interface to the local credit management team. Monitor aging of receivables and support collection of overdues via development of payment plans Support LBM/Marketing/Sales in the design of commercial schemes by providing accurate information and developing comparative models for profit analysis Design SOP for commercial scheme pay-out and ensure proper records are kept for audit purposes Strategic controlling: Support financial evaluation as well as development and implementation of strategic projects (e.g. business models, go-to-market approach, make-or-buy decisions, investment decisions, portfolio management) Support strategy development. Plan, forecast and monitor capital expenditure Drive and train mindset of value based management across the organization Support value based decision making throughout the organization targeting sustainable optimization of absolute Market Performance. Perform ad-hoc analyses based on operational business steering requirements. Execute specific action plan to reduce dysfunctional stock (aging, no & slow mover). Strictly follow the inventory guideline process for inventory write-off and stock taking procedures [SC1] [RB2] . Oversee and coordinate with the Supply Chain team for all SC related tasks. Corporate Governance and Compliance: Go-to-person and increase awareness around compliance, internal guidelines and processes. Support design and/or improvement of internal controls Support internal audits and track follow-ups. Accountable for contract management. Responsible for project mangement specifically with the Differentiated Steering Minimum Education and Qualifications Required Bachelor Degree in Finance / Accounting Master’s Degree in Finance / Accounting is an advantage Working Experience: Minimum 3 years of professional experience in Controlling Technical & Professional Knowledge Advanced finance and accounting skills, proficiency in interpretation and analysis of financial reports. Strong knowledge of demand planning, inventory & distribution planning process APO DP & SAP knowledge is beneficial. Advanced skills in MS Office applications. Good command in English. Good communication skills, team player. All applications will be treated confidentially Only short-listed candidates will be interviewed Job Description Footer:
